articles.title: 2022 corruption case: HC rejects ex-minister Sunder Sham Arora’s plea for fresh probe
articles.description: The high court observed that it was too far-fetched to accept that a subordinate officer would always be biased in investigating a complaint filed by his superior

articles.title: Turkish proverb of the day: 'Kill the brave man, but do not deny him his due' — Reminding us that fairness means acknowledging merit even in conflict or defeat
articles.description: The Turkish proverb, 'Kill the brave man, but do not deny him his due,' emphasizes the importance of recognizing the courage and merit of an opponent, even in moments of competition. This age-old wisdom speaks volumes about the need for fairness and integrity in both personal and professional interactions. It teaches us that true strength lies in appreciating the virtues of others, fostering growth and mutual respect within society.

articles.description: U.S. Air Force Maj. Jason Watson was arrested Wednesday while demonstrating at the Capitol for the impeachment of President Trump. Capitol police said they arrested Maj. Watson for demonstrating on the Capitol steps, which is against the law unless the demonstrator is with a member of Congress. Maj. Watson was initially accompanied ...

articles.title: Tamil Nadu moves SC over Madras HC order on cow slaughter ban
articles.description: The Tamil Nadu government has moved the Supreme Court challenging a Madras HC order that directed the southern state to impose a complete ban on cow slaughter.

articles.title: Ahead of UP elections, SP chief Akhilesh Yadav releases QR code for crowdfunding
articles.description: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav launched a QR code for crowdfunding, 'PDA Swabhiman Sahyog,' on his birthday. This initiative aims for transparent fund collection and deeper connection with ground-level workers, especially from the PDA (Pichda, Dalit, Alpsankhyak) community. The party seeks minimum ₹20 contributions to boost public participation and strengthen its organization, echoing a socialist formula to reduce reliance on industrialist funding.

articles.title: Chennai metrorail ridership rebounds in June after school, colleges reopen
articles.description: Chennai: Chennai Metro Rail ridership rebounded in June after schools and colleges reopened, with an average of 3.2 lakh passengers travelling daily. .

articles.title: Funds received before Ram Temple construction unaccounted for: UP Congress in-charge RP Gautam
articles.description: Questioning the functioning of the Ram Temple Trust, Gautam said: “Who constituted the temple trust and who inaugurated the temple? If credit is taken for all these things, why should people not also believe the allegations of theft?”

articles.title: Man held for harassing woman, assaulting husband at KCBT
articles.description: Chennai: A 23-year-old man was arrested after he harassed a newly married woman and assaulted her husband at the Kilambakkam bus terminus on Tuesday n.

articles.title: CPI(M) opposes Centre’s nod for mining in Agency areas
articles.description: CPI(M) opposes Centre's mining decision in Agency areas, citing tribal rights violations and potential unrest over mineral access.

articles.title: JPC report on 130th Amendment Bill to keep removal clause for PM, CMs
articles.description: The JPC examining the 130th Constitution Amendment Bill is likely to retain the debated clause mandating the automatic removal of a PM or CM if arrested and held in custody for 30 days. The report is expected to be adopted on July 17.

articles.title: APMAS has empowered millions of women through SHG movement, says Governor
articles.description: Governor highlights APMAS's role in empowering women through SHGs, marking 25 years of impactful community development in India.

articles.title: Bill To Sack Jailed Ministers Back On Track, Likely In Monsoon Session
articles.description: The Opposition has objected to the bill, arguing that it is undemocratic and anti-federal.

articles.title: Peace in Lebanon only by eliminating terror groups, India gets it: Israel
articles.description: Israel's Deputy FM Sharren Haskel said peace in Lebanon is only possible by eliminating terrorist groups like Hezbollah, drawing a parallel to India's fight against cross-border terrorism and affirming Israel will act against Hezbollah if needed.

articles.title: BJP slams Telangana CM for his remarks on party chief Nitin Nabin
articles.description: BJP criticizes Telangana CM Revanth Reddy's remarks on party chief Nitin Nabin, calling them arrogant and politically damaging.

articles.title: WATCH: Eggs, Vegetables Hurled At Mahua Moitra; TMC MP Accuses 'BJP Goons', Says Police Stood By
articles.description: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra alleged that BJP workers hurled eggs and vegetables at the eatery where she was meeting party workers in West Bengal. Videos from both inside and outside the premises have gone viral, with one showing protesters throwing eggs and raising slogans, while another, filmed by Moitra, captures eggs striking the windows. In posts on X and Facebook Live, the Krishnanagar MP accused the West Bengal Police of remaining passive despite being informed about the gathering. She claimed protesters stood outside for over an hour while police watched. The BJP did not immediately respond to the allegations. Police have launched an investigation into the incident, and no injuries were reported.

articles.title: Union Government Moves To Evict Delhi Gymkhana Club, Cites Defence Use And 1928 Lease Deed | News18
articles.description: | The Union government has initiated proceedings to evict the Delhi Gymkhana Club from its 27.3-acre Safdarjung Road premises, stating that the land is required for defence infrastructure, public security, and other national purposes. The Centre has invoked the original 1928 lease deed while seeking possession of the property, marking a significant development in a long-standing land dispute. The move is expected to spark legal scrutiny and debate over heritage property use, club privileges, and government land requirements in central Delhi. n18oc_breaking-news News18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube

articles.title: Internet labels Harshad Chopra ‘homophobic’ after his conversation with Shreya Kalra on Lock Upp: ‘Way too cringe’
articles.description: Recently, a video of Harshad Chopda having a conversation with Shreya Kalra has surfaced on social media, which has sparked conversations.

articles.title: After Citizenship Setback, Trump Could Use New Tools To Expedite Deportations
articles.description: Team Trump could move forward with a process known as "expedited removal".

articles.title: Fight over Hormuz control: Iran says the strait is ‘non-negotiable’ as US ally sides with Mojtaba?
articles.description: The Strait of Hormuz has become the center of a fresh geopolitical confrontation as Iran insists its control over the strategic waterway is "non-negotiable." While the U.S. opposes any shipping fees, reports suggest Oman has proposed a new framework that could reshape maritime transit after months of conflict. Iranian officials argue the era of free passage is over, while Washington insists commercial navigation must remain unrestricted. Is the latest dispute the next flashpoint in the U.S.-Iran standoff?

articles.title: Evacuations after Putin’s plan to seize Kyiv leaks; Russia-Ukraine war about to enter a new phase?
articles.description: Ukraine has ordered fresh evacuations after Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi claimed that Russian President Vladimir Putin instructed the General Staff to prepare plans for a renewed offensive, including a possible push toward Kyiv. Ukrainian officials believe Russia could launch a new operation from the Bryansk region while continuing pressure along multiple fronts. The developments have raised fears that the Russia-Ukraine war may be entering a dangerous new phase. Watch the latest updates, military assessments, evacuation orders, and what this could mean for the battlefield and the wider geopolitical situation.

articles.title: Raghav Chadha cried foul over AI posts and the Delhi HC stepped in
articles.description: Delhi High Court orders the removal of five defamatory AI-manipulated social media posts targeting AAP MP Raghav Chadha. Read the full legal update here.

articles.title: Stitching the Value Chain: How PM MITRA parks are rewriting India's textile destiny
articles.description: India's textile sector, a vital economic pillar, is undergoing a transformative shift with the PM MITRA scheme. This initiative aims to overcome historical fragmentation by creating integrated textile parks, fostering a 'Farm to Foreign' value chain. The scheme is poised to boost exports, create millions of jobs, and enhance global competitiveness while ensuring social equity.

articles.title: AIDSA Suspends Dentist Dr. Muskan Soni for 5 Years Over Alleged Remarks on Ketan Agarwal's Death
articles.description: AIDSA has suspended dentist Dr. Muskan Soni for five years after she allegedly made offensive remarks about the death of Ketan Agarwal in a viral Instagram Story.Following public outrage, Dr. Soni issued an apology, saying she never intended to joke about such a sensitive matter. The suspension comes as police continue investigating the murder of Ketan Agarwal.

articles.title: UPTET 2026: Yogi Adityanath orders separate TET for in-service teachers ahead of exam; asks officials to ensure smooth conduct
articles.description: The Uttar Pradesh government has directed the UPESSC to conduct a separate Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) for in-service teachers, aiming to provide them with more opportunities to meet eligibility requirements. The decision comes ahead of UPTET 2026, which will be held from July 2 to 4, as the state intensifies preparations to ensure a fair, transparent and smooth examination for nearly 20 lakh candidates.

articles.title: Congress Demands Privilege Proceedings Against Rajnath Singh, Says He 'Misled Parliament' on Op Sindoor Soldier Deaths
articles.description: The Congress party has accused Rajnath Singh of lying in Parliament regarding the deaths of soldiers during Operation Sindoor, demanding his resignation.

articles.title: ‘We hit Iran twice, will do it again if...’: Netanyahu issues fresh warning over nuclear programme
articles.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that Israel could strike Iran again, saying the country has acted twice before and would not hesitate to launch a third operation to stop Tehran from acquiring nuclear weapons.

articles.title: US Lifts Sanctions On Four Indian Entities Linked To Russia-Related Order
articles.description: The US Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) announced the removals as part of its latest update to the SDN List.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu moves Supreme Court against Madras High Court order banning cow slaughter
articles.description: According to court records, the State has filed a Special Leave Petition (SLP) through its Secretary against the May 27 judgment.

articles.title: Harsh Goenka shares '3–30–300 rule of life'; says choose your circle wisely
articles.description: Industrialist Harsh Goenka shared his "3–30–300 Rule of Life" on social media, emphasizing the importance of choosing influential people wisely. He advised that three individuals shape you, thirty broaden your thinking, and three hundred expand your network. Goenka also shared a heartwarming video of a father using a tactile board to help his blind son experience a football match, showcasing ingenuity and dedication.

articles.title: JAAC Leader Shaukat Mir's Arrest Triggers Widespread Protests Across PoK, Demonstrations Reach UK
articles.description: Demonstrations were organised in UK, where members of the Kashmiri diaspora expressed solidarity with the movement and condemned the arrest of Shaukat Nawaz Mir.

articles.title: 'Bullying was ignored, Bible readings were routine': Parent's post on Bengaluru international school is viral
articles.description: The account, shared anonymously on Reddit, was presented as a caution to families considering relocating to India with school-age children. The parent urged others to speak with families already associated with prospective schools before making enrolment decisions, arguing that marketing material and university admission records do not necessarily reflect students' day-to-day experiences.

articles.title: Iran, Oman advance plan to make vessels pay for Strait of Hormuz passage despite US objection
articles.description: Middle East News: Iran and Oman are advancing their proposal for mandatory transit fees in the Strait of Hormuz, despite US President Trump's strong objections. This contentious move could reshape shipping practices in one of the world's most crucial waterways.

articles.title: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy Criticizes Mamata Banerjee Sparks Political Row in Congress | News18
articles.description: Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy has sparked a political controversy after reportedly criticizing West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee, leading to internal tensions within the Congress party. The remarks have triggered reactions from political leaders and intensified debate over party unity and leadership coordination. In this video, we break down what was said, why it matters, and how it could impact Congress politics and opposition dynamics in India. n18oc_breaking-newsNews18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube

articles.title: Drones, AI and nuclear submarines: Inside UK’s £298 billion plan to make Britain war-ready
articles.description: The UK is embarking on a transformative defence initiative, investing £298 billion over the next four years. Prime Minister Keir Starmer unveiled this far-reaching plan, which emphasizes the importance of drones, artificial intelligence, and nuclear resilience in response to increasing global tensions. With a focus on modernizing the army and substantial investments in new submarines and combat aircraft, the plan aims to proactively deter threats.

articles.title: Upholding pension relief for employee, court fines Odisha Rs 1 lakh over delayed appeal
articles.description: The case before the Orissa High Court arose from the state’s challenge to an order granting pension benefits to a man who had worked for over 41 years, but learnt later that his junior’s services had been regularised.

articles.title: Days after Pakistan's airstrikes, Afghanistan launches strikes on ISIS targets in Balochistan
articles.description: Afghanistan's Taliban forces conducted strikes in Pakistan's Balochistan province, targeting alleged Islamic State bases. This follows Pakistan's recent airstrikes in Afghanistan, which killed civilians and were condemned by India as a "blatant act of aggression." Tensions are escalating after a period of relative calm, with intermittent border clashes and air strikes claiming dozens of lives recently.

articles.title: Court validates customary divorce, allows 69-year-old woman father’s Army pension
articles.description: The Kerala High Court said that a customary divorce executed more than four decades ago cannot be ignored merely because a formal family court decree came after the death of the pensioner's parents.

articles.title: Phil Weiser beats Michael Bennet, wins Democratic nomination for governor in Colorado
articles.description: Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser won the Democratic nomination for governor of the state Tuesday, beating out U.S. Sen. Michael Bennet in a race partly centered around which candidate was better at challenging President Trump. Weiser became attorney general in 2019, after serving in the Justice Department under the presidential administrations ...

articles.title: ‘Entered Twice, Will Do It Again’: Netanyahu Warns Of 'Third Strike' On Iran If Needed
articles.description: The Israeli leader also reiterated that Israeli forces would remain deployed in southern Lebanon as long as they viewed the Iran-backed Hezbollah as a threat.

articles.title: Indus Waters Treaty remains 'valid, binding and operative': Pakistan Deputy PM Dar
articles.description: Pakistan vehemently rejects India's revocation of the Indus Waters Treaty, asserting its continued validity and binding nature. Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar emphasized the treaty's role in regional peace, warning of severe consequences if Pakistan is deprived of its allocated waters. Former Foreign Minister Bilawal Bhutto Zardari proposed an international convention against weaponizing waterways, highlighting the Indus River's critical importance for stability.

articles.title: Bangladesh Hindu Temple Priest Abducted, Stripped And Assaulted By Unknown People
articles.description: In Bangladesh, a Hindu law student and temple priest named Subhash Deuri was reportedly abducted and assaulted for money.

articles.title: Economic security, AI, defence in focus as PM Takaichi set to arrive in Delhi for India-Japan Summit
articles.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi will visit India from July 1 to 3 to attend the 16th India-Japan Annual Summit and hold talks aimed at strengthening bilateral cooperation

articles.title: 'No Intrusions': Kiren Rijiju Dismisses Claims Of Chinese Encroachment In Arunachal Pradesh
articles.description: Rijiju said there has been no Chinese intrusion into Arunachal, while the Army too dismissed reports of PLA encroachment as "incorrect and without any basis."
